info.title: SWP391 JGMS API
info.description:
Jira & GitHub Management System — API for frontend integration.

**Auth flow:** POST `/api/auth/login` → get `accessToken` → click 'Authorize' button above → paste token.

post.summary: Create a new user (admin, lecturer, or student).

get.summary: Get all users.

put.summary:
Update user information.
Accepts email or numeric user ID.

get.summary: Get user by email or numeric user ID.

delete.summary: Delete user. Accepts email or numeric user ID.

get.summary: Get users by role (admin, lecturer, student).

Search users by name or email (partial match, case-insensitive).
Optionally filter by role (admin, lecturer, student).
Useful for finding users without knowing their numeric ID.
Example: GET /api/admin/users/search?q=nguyen&role=student

patch.summary: Set user status (active/inactive). Accepts email or numeric user ID.

post.summary: Create a new student group.

get.summary: Get all student groups.

put.summary: Update student group. Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

get.summary: Get student group by group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

delete.summary: Delete student group. Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

get.summary:
Get all active lecturers.
Use this to populate the lecturer picker when creating a group.

Get all active students who are NOT currently in any group.
Use this to populate the student picker (leader + members) when creating or filling a group.

post.summary:
Add one or more students to a group in a single request.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.
Each student identifier can be an email address or a numeric user ID.
Returns a summary of added students and any failures (with reasons).

delete.summary:
Clear all members from a group. Only allowed when the group's project is marked as completed.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

Remove student from group.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.
Accepts student numeric user ID or email address.

post.summary:
Configure GitHub integration for a user — sets their github_username.
Accepts email or numeric user ID.

delete.summary:
Remove GitHub integration for a user — clears their github_username.
Accepts email or numeric user ID.

post.summary:
Configure Jira integration for a user — sets their jira_account_id.
Accepts email or numeric user ID.

delete.summary:
Remove Jira integration for a user — clears their jira_account_id.
Accepts email or numeric user ID.

get.summary: Get all users with their GitHub/Jira integration status.

post.summary:
Test integration connectivity (GitHub or Jira).
Pass integrationType = "GitHub" or "Jira".

Create a project for a group. Each group can only have one project.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID in the route.

get.summary:
Get the project for a specific group.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

put.summary: Update a project. Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID to find the project.

delete.summary: Delete a project. Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

get.summary: Get all projects.

post.summary:
Configure GitHub integration for a project.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

Rotates the AES encryption key for all stored GitHub API tokens.
            
How to use when key is compromised:
1. Generate a new 32-byte Base64 key (e.g. via: openssl rand -base64 32)
2. Call this endpoint with the new key — all tokens are re-encrypted atomically
3. Update the Encryption__Key environment variable on Render to the new key
4. Redeploy the app
            
The old key must still be active (in env var) when calling this endpoint.
Register a new student account.

Register a new lecturer account.
Does not require student code, GitHub username, or Jira account ID.
Login with email and password. Returns a JWT access token.

Configure Jira integration for a project (Admin only).
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

get.summary:
Get Jira integration configuration for a project.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

put.summary:
Update Jira integration configuration (Admin only).
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

delete.summary:
Delete Jira integration configuration (Admin only).
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

get.summary:
Test Jira connection (Admin only).
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.
Get all Jira integrations (Admin only).

post.summary:
Sync issues from Jira to database (Admin or Team Leader).
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

get.summary:
Get sync status for a group's project.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

Get all synced Jira issues for a group's project (role-based filtering).
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.
Get single issue details by issue key (e.g. "SWP391-123").
Get all groups assigned to the current lecturer.

Get details of a specific group assigned to the lecturer.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

put.summary:
Update a group assigned to the lecturer.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

get.summary:
Get all members in a group assigned to the lecturer.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

post.summary:
Add one or more students to a group assigned to the lecturer.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.
Each student identifier can be an email address or a numeric user ID.
Returns a summary of added students and any failures (with reasons).

delete.summary:
Remove a student from a group assigned to the lecturer.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.
Accepts student numeric user ID or email address.

get.summary:
Get all requirements for the assigned group's project.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

Get all tasks for the assigned group's project.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

Get progress reports for the assigned group's project.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

Export a progress report for the assigned group's project.
Supported formats: word, pdf.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

Get aggregated GitHub commit statistics for the assigned group's project.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.
Get all tasks assigned to the current student.
Get a specific task by ID (must be assigned to you).

post.summary:
Generate an auto commit line suggestion from either task ID or Jira issue key.
Supports optional conventional-commit customization and returns Git/GitHub commands.

put.summary:
Update the status of a task assigned to you.
Accepted status values (case-insensitive, flexible formatting):
- "todo", "To Do", "to do", "to_do"
- "in_progress", "In Progress", "in progress", "in-progress"
- "done", "Done"

post.summary:
Mark a task as completed — sets CompletedAt timestamp.
Only works for tasks assigned to you.

get.summary:
Get personal task and commit statistics overview.
Shows completed vs pending tasks, commit history, contribution metrics.
Get task statistics grouped by status (todo, in_progress, done).
Get personal task statistics (completion and progress metrics).
Get personal commit statistics (commits this week/month, averages).
Get personal commit history timeline from GitHub.
Get current student's profile information.

put.summary:
Update basic profile information (phone, GitHub username, Jira account).
Students can only update specific fields, not role or status.

get.summary:
Get SRS documents for a project, identified by group code (e.g. "SE1234") or group ID.
Any group member can access. For leader-only SRS management, see the Group SRS Documents section.
Get a specific SRS document by ID.

Download SRS document file.
Security: Validates file path to prevent directory traversal attacks.

Get the group the current student belongs to.
Returns 404 if the student has not been assigned to a group yet.
Includes group details, project info, and the list of team members.

Get project details for a group. Leader only.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

Get progress reports for a group project. Leader only.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

post.summary: Create a new progress report for a group project. Leader only.

get.summary:
Get guided template for creating progress reports. Leader only.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

Get all requirements for a group project.
Any group member can view; only leaders can create/edit/delete.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

post.summary:
Create a requirement and push it to Jira as a new issue. Leader only.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

put.summary:
Update a requirement and sync changes back to Jira. Leader only.
Accepts group code or group ID, and requirement ID.

delete.summary: Delete a requirement and remove the corresponding issue from Jira. Leader only.

put.summary: Reorder requirements hierarchy (Epic → Story → Task → Sub-task). Leader only.

post.summary:
Bulk-import all synced Jira issues as requirements. Leader only.
Skips issues that are already linked to an existing requirement.
Run a Jira sync first to ensure issues are up to date.

get.summary:
Get all tasks for a group project. Leader only.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.

post.summary: Create a task, assign a member, set deadline and optionally link to Jira. Leader only.
Create a task pre-populated from a synced Jira issue key (e.g. "SWP391-5"). Leader only.

put.summary:
Edit a task: update status, assignee, description, priority, due date. Leader only.
Changes are synced back to Jira if the task is linked to a Jira issue.

delete.summary: Delete a task. Also removes the linked Jira issue if integration is configured. Leader only.

post.summary: Assign a task to a group member. Leader only.

put.summary:
Move a task's linked Jira issue to a different sprint. Leader only.
Use sprintId = 0 to move the issue back to the backlog.
Link a task to a requirement. Creates a "Relates" issue link in Jira if applicable. Leader only.

post.summary: Push all local changes (tasks + requirements) to Jira in bulk. Leader only.

get.summary:
Get all SRS documents for a group's project. Leader only.
Accepts group code (e.g. "SE1234") or numeric group ID.
Get a single SRS document by ID with all included requirements. Leader only.

put.summary:
Update SRS document metadata. Leader only.
Set status to "published" to finalize the document.

post.summary: Generate an SRS document from existing requirements. Leader only.

get.summary: Download the SRS document as an HTML file. Leader only.
Download the SRS document as a Word-compatible (.doc) file. Leader only.

post.summary:
Regenerate the requirements snapshot of an existing SRS document. Leader only.
Replaces all previously included requirements with a fresh selection.
Optionally auto-imports new Jira issues as requirements before regenerating.
Does NOT create a new SRS version — use the generate endpoint for that.
